records.ety: [L. particula, dim. of pars, gen partis, a part: cf. F. particule. See Part, and cf. Parcel.]

defs: A minute part or portion of matter; a morsel; a little bit; an atom; a jot; as, a particle of sand, of wood, of dust.
quotes: The small size of atoms which unite To make the smallest particle of light.

defs: Any very small portion or part; the smallest portion; as, he has not a particle of patriotism or virtue.
quotes: The houses had not given their commissioners authority in the least particle to recede.

defs: A crumb or little piece of consecrated host.
The smaller hosts distributed in the communion of the laity.
A subordinate word that is never inflected (a preposition, conjunction, interjection); or a word that can not be used except in compositions; as, ward in backward, ly in lovely.
